title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: arcopt (Adaptive Regularization using Cubics R) optimization package implementing cubic regularization methods (ARC) local optimization problems common statistics applied research. Primary Users: R users (statisticians, applied researchers) Problem Scale: Small medium (typically 2-500 parameters) GitHub: https://github.com/marcus-waldman/arcopt

title: CLAUDE.md
text: optimizer implements hierarchical 4-layer system: Orchestration Layer: Main loop (Algorithm 1) controls iteration flow dispatches sub-algorithms Core Solvers Layer: Newton step computation (Algorithm 1a) eigendecomposition cubic solver (Algorithm 5a) Adaptation Layer: Convergence checks (Algorithm 0), σ (sigma) regularization updates (Algorithms 2a/2b), momentum (Algorithm 3) Safeguards Layer: Indefiniteness handling (Algorithms 6a/6b), box constraint truncation (Algorithm 7), linear equality constraint handling (Algorithm 8)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Hessian-Centric: Accurate second derivative information prioritized. Hessian sources order preference: analytic > automatic differentiation > finite differences > quasi-Newton approximation. QN-ARC mode available via use_qn = TRUE. Robust Default: Single entry point arcopt(x0, fn, gr, hess) sensible defaults; users need manually tune regularization select solvers Saddle Point Escape: Cubic regularization naturally handles negative curvature without explicit eigenvector computation Constraint Handling: Box constraints via step truncation; linear equality constraints via null-space transformation

title: CLAUDE.md
text: design/design-principles.qmd: Design philosophy, target user profile, problem characteristics, core principles design/pseudocode.qmd: Complete algorithmic specifications (8 algorithm groups) system flowchart detailed pseudocode design/literature-review.qmd: Academic references theoretical foundations literature/consensus_reviews/: Topic-specific reviews regularization parameters, failure modes, comparisons ARC implementations

title: CLAUDE.md
text: research project early development stages. repository currently contains: - Documentation design specifications (implementation yet) - Git history yet initialized main branch - Focus algorithm specification coding

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Follow pseudocode specifications design/pseudocode.qmd exactly—implementation-ready Maintain Hessian-centric philosophy: Default interface requires Hessian function; provide finite-difference wrapper convenience option Prioritize robustness: Include automatic indefiniteness detection, adaptive σ adjustment, numerical safeguards Test pathological problems: Ill-conditioned, nonconvex, indefinite Hessian, saddle point, ridge maximum cases Reference design principles making architectural decisions—existing decisions well-justified

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Based literature review design documents: - Core cubic regularization solver (Algorithm 5a) eigendecomposition ✓ - Adaptive regularization updates (Algorithms 2a/2b) σ adjustment ✓ - Indefiniteness handling negative curvature ✓ - Constraint handling (Algorithms 7-8) box linear equality constraints ✓ - Momentum acceleration (Algorithm 3) ARCm variant ✓

title: CLAUDE.md
text: maintain focus core use case (statisticians, 2-500 parameters, analytic Hessians): Removed favor eigendecomposition: - LDL-based cubic solver (see design/historical/ldl-solver.qmd) Now implemented (Algorithm 4 variants): - SR1, BFGS, L-SR1, L-BFGS quasi-Newton updates - Hybrid routing (BFGS → SR1 → Powell damping) robustness - L-Hybrid limited-memory routing logic Deferred future releases: - ARCqK multi-shift CG-Lanczos solver (Algorithm 5b) n > 500 - Matrix-free optimization via hess_vec interface See design/scalable-arcs.qmd preserved implementations design rationale.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: install.packages(c("devtools", "usethis", "roxygen2", "testthat",                    "pkgdown", "lintr", "styler", "covr", "Rcpp"))

title: CLAUDE.md
text: committing code, always run sequence: devtools::load_all() - Load package testing devtools::document() - Update documentation roxygen2 comments lintr::lint_package() - CRITICAL: Check Tidyverse style compliance styler::style_pkg() - Auto-fix formatting issues devtools::test() - Run tests devtools::check() - Full R CMD check (must pass 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes) modifying C++ code src/: - Run Rcpp::compileAttributes() regenerate RcppExports.R/cpp - run devtools::document() update NAMESPACE

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package strictly follows Tidyverse Style Guide Enforced .lintr configuration: - snake_case naming functions variables (e.g., solve_cubic, solveCubic) - 120 character line length maximum - 2-space indentation (never tabs) - roxygen2 documentation required exported functions - browser(), print() debugging statements production code Common violations avoid: - CamelCase PascalCase names (use snake_case) - Lines 120 characters (break long lines) - Missing incomplete roxygen2 documentation - Trailing whitespace

title: CLAUDE.md
text: every commit: - [ ] lintr::lint_package() shows errors - [ ] devtools::check() passes (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes) - [ ] tests pass devtools::test() - [ ] roxygen2 documentation current - [ ] debugging code (browser(), print()) commits

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package must always ready CRAN submission: DESCRIPTION file: - Title title case - Description 2+ sentences, doesn’t start package name - dependencies declared Imports/Suggests - Rcpp Imports LinkingTo Code requirements: - exported functions complete roxygen2 documentation - Examples documentation actually run (use \donttest{}) - absolute file paths (use system.file() package files) - Proper importFrom() :: external functions Rcpp-specific: - editing C++ files, run Rcpp::compileAttributes() committing - RcppExports.R RcppExports.cpp committed (tracked Git) - Makevars Makevars.win configure compilation properly

title: CLAUDE.md
text: GitHub Actions automatically enforce standards every push: - R-CMD-check.yaml: Tests 5 platforms (macOS, Windows, Linux × multiple R versions) - lint.yaml: Fails lintr detects style violations - test-coverage.yaml: Reports code coverage codecov.io - pkgdown.yaml: Builds deploys package website workflows must pass merging PRs (configure GitHub branch protection).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: implementing algorithms design/pseudocode.qmd: 1. Start pure R implementation (easier debug) 2. Write comprehensive tests first (test-driven development) 3. Profile code profvis::profvis() identify bottlenecks 4. Move hot paths C++ profiling confirms need 5. Maintain equivalence tests R C++ implementations

title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
text: Robust nonlinear optimization R optim() fails ill-conditioned model, arcopt designed succeed. uses Adaptive Regularization Cubics (ARC) handle indefinite Hessians escape saddle points automatically.

title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
code: # Install from GitHub pak::pak("marcus-waldman/arcopt")  # Or with devtools devtools::install_github("marcus-waldman/arcopt")

title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
code: library(arcopt)  # Rosenbrock function - a classic difficult optimization problem result <- arcopt(   x0 = c(-1.2, 1),   fn = function(x) (1 - x[1])^2 + 100 * (x[2] - x[1]^2)^2,   gr = function(x) c(     -2 * (1 - x[1]) - 400 * x[1] * (x[2] - x[1]^2),     200 * (x[2] - x[1]^2)   ),   hess = function(x) matrix(c(     1200 * x[1]^2 - 400 * x[2] + 2, -400 * x[1],     -400 * x[1], 200   ), 2, 2) )  result$par #> [1] 1 1

previous_headings: Two Modes: Exact Hessian vs Quasi-Newton
title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
text: Best computing Hessian expensive unavailable. Uses BFGS/SR1 approximations.
code: # Just provide fn and gr - no Hessian needed result <- arcopt(x0, fn, gr, control = list(use_qn = TRUE))

title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
text: Tested 8 standard functions 10 starting points (80 test cases): BFGS Quasi-Newton achieves 100% convergence exact Hessian 26% iterations zero Hessian evaluations.

title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
code: # Optimize with bounds: 0 <= x <= 0.5 result <- arcopt(   x0 = c(0.1, 0.1),   fn = fn, gr = gr, hess = hess,   lower = c(0, 0),   upper = c(0.5, 0.5) )

title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
text: Standard optimizers struggle Hessian indefinite nearly singular—common : Maximum likelihood complex likelihoods Bayesian posterior modes (MAP estimation) Nonlinear mixed effects models model poor identifiability Cubic regularization automatically handles negative curvature without line searches trust-region adjustments. provably escapes saddle points converges local minima.

title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
text: Instead quadratic model used Newton’s method: ARC adds cubic term: cubic term prevents unbounded steps Hessian negative eigenvalues.

title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
code: arcopt(x0, fn, gr, hess,   control = list(     # Quasi-Newton mode (no Hessian needed)     use_qn = TRUE,     qn_method = "hybrid",    # or "bfgs", "sr1", "lbfgs", "lsr1", "lhybrid"      # Convergence tolerances     gtol_abs = 1e-5,         # Gradient norm     maxit = 1000,            # Max iterations      # Momentum (enabled by default, helps ill-conditioned problems)     use_momentum = TRUE,      # Diagnostics     trace = 1                # Print progress   ) )

title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
text: arcopt local optimizer. Use something else : Global optimization: Use DEoptim, GenSA, nloptr global algorithms Derivative-free: Use nloptr COBYLA dfoptim large scale (n > 10,000): Use lbfgs sparse methods Linear/Quadratic programming: Use lpSolve, quadprog Best : 2-500 parameters, nonconvex objectives, need robust convergence.

title: arcopt - Adaptive Regularization using Cubics
code: result <- arcopt(x0, fn, gr, hess)  result$par          # Optimal parameters result$value        # Optimal function value result$gradient     # Gradient at solution result$hessian      # Hessian at solution (if provided) result$converged    # TRUE if converged result$iterations   # Number of iterations result$evaluations  # List: fn, gr, hess counts result$message      # Why it stopped

what: Apply Box Constraints to Step — apply_box_constraints
title: Apply Box Constraints to Step — apply_box_constraints
text: Truncates proposed step ensure new iterate stays within box constraints, projects numerical safety.

title: Apply Box Constraints to Step — apply_box_constraints
code: apply_box_constraints(x_current, s, lower, upper)

title: Apply Box Constraints to Step — apply_box_constraints
text: x_current Current iterate s Proposed unconstrained step lower Lower bounds (use -Inf unbounded) upper Upper bounds (use Inf unbounded)

title: Apply Box Constraints to Step — apply_box_constraints
text: List components: s_bounded Truncated step respects bounds x_new New iterate projected feasible region

title: Apply Box Constraints to Step — apply_box_constraints
text: implements Algorithm 7a design specification. approach: Compute maximum feasible step length alpha_max Truncate step: s_bounded = alpha_max * s Project x_k + s_bounded box numerical safety

what: arcopt: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics for Optimization — arcopt-package
title: arcopt: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics for Optimization — arcopt-package
text: Implements cubic regularization methods (ARC) local optimization problems common statistics applied research. Provides robust handling ill-conditioned, nonconvex, indefinite Hessian problems automatic saddle point escape. Supports box constraints; linear equality constraints planned future release.

title: arcopt: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics for Optimization — arcopt-package
text: Maintainer: Marcus Waldman .email@domain.com

what: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
text: Minimizes nonlinear objective function using Adaptive Regularization Cubics (ARC). Designed robust optimization ill-conditioned, nonconvex, indefinite Hessian problems common statistical applications.

title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
code: arcopt(   x0,   fn,   gr,   hess = NULL,   ...,   lower = rep(-Inf, length(x0)),   upper = rep(Inf, length(x0)),   control = list() )

title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
text: x0 Numeric vector initial parameter values (length Q). fn Function computes objective function value. take numeric vector length Q return scalar. gr Function computes gradient. take numeric vector length Q return numeric vector length Q. Required. hess Function computes Hessian matrix. take numeric vector length Q return Q×Q symmetric matrix. Required. ... Additional arguments passed fn, gr, hess. lower Numeric vector lower bounds (length Q). Use -Inf unbounded parameters. Default: -Inf. upper Numeric vector upper bounds (length Q). Use Inf unbounded parameters. Default: Inf. control List control parameters (see Details).

title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
text: list components: par: Optimal parameter vector value: Optimal function value gradient: Gradient optimum hessian: Hessian optimum (hess provided) converged: Logical, whether convergence criteria met iterations: Number iterations performed evaluations: List fn, gr, hess evaluation counts message: Convergence message

title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
text: ARC algorithm iteratively minimizes cubic regularization model: $$m_k(s) = f_k + g_k^T s + \frac{1}{2} s^T H_k s + \frac{\sigma_k}{3} \|s\|^3$$ \(\sigma_k\) adaptively adjusted based model accuracy.

title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
text: ARC critically depends accurate curvature information. hess argument required. convenience, finite-difference Hessians can computed automatically control$use_fd = TRUE, analytic Hessians strongly recommended best performance.

title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
text: control list accepts: maxit: Maximum iterations (default: 1000) ftol_abs: Absolute function tolerance (default: 1e-8) gtol_abs: Absolute gradient norm tolerance (default: 1e-5) xtol_abs: Absolute step size tolerance (default: 1e-8) sigma0: Initial regularization parameter (default: 1.0) eta1: Acceptance threshold step (default: 0.1) eta2: successful step threshold (default: 0.9) gamma1: Regularization decrease factor (default: 0.5) gamma2: Regularization increase factor (default: 2.0) cubic_solver: Solver selection: "auto" (recommended) "eigen" (default: "auto"). Auto-selection uses eigendecomposition (Algorithm 5a) robust handling indefinite Hessians hard cases. use_momentum: Enable momentum acceleration (default: FALSE). Implements Gao et al. (2022) ARCm recursive momentum bisection search monotonicity. recommended known ill-conditioned problems - well-conditioned problems, bisection overhead may negate iteration savings. Empirically shows mixed results: can dramatically reduce iterations problems increasing others. momentum_tau: Maximum momentum parameter (default: 0.5, Gao's τ) momentum_alpha1: Linear step scaling constant (default: 0.1, Gao's α₁) momentum_alpha2: Quadratic step scaling constant (default: 1.0, Gao's α₂) trace: Print iteration progress (default: FALSE) use_qn: Use quasi-Newton Hessian approximation (default: FALSE). TRUE, routes arcopt_qn uses QN updates instead exact Hessians. See qn_method available QN methods. qn_method: QN update method - "sr1", "bfgs", "lbfgs", "lsr1" (default: "sr1"). used use_qn = TRUE.

title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
code: # \donttest{ # Rosenbrock function rosenbrock <- function(x) (1 - x[1])^2 + 100 * (x[2] - x[1]^2)^2  rosenbrock_gr <- function(x) {   c(-2 * (1 - x[1]) - 400 * x[1] * (x[2] - x[1]^2),     200 * (x[2] - x[1]^2)) }  rosenbrock_hess <- function(x) {   matrix(c(     1200 * x[1]^2 - 400 * x[2] + 2, -400 * x[1],     -400 * x[1], 200   ), 2, 2) }  result <- arcopt(   x0 = c(-1.2, 1),   fn = rosenbrock,   gr = rosenbrock_gr,   hess = rosenbrock_hess )  print(result$par)      # Should be near c(1, 1) #> [1] 0.9999994 0.9999989 print(result$value)    # Should be near 0 #> [1] 3.138905e-13 # }

title: Quasi-Newton ARC Optimizer — arcopt_qn
text: Minimizes nonlinear objective function using Adaptive Regularization Cubics quasi-Newton Hessian approximations. require exact Hessian function.

title: Quasi-Newton ARC Optimizer — arcopt_qn
code: arcopt_qn(   x0,   fn,   gr,   hess = NULL,   ...,   lower = rep(-Inf, length(x0)),   upper = rep(Inf, length(x0)),   control = list() )

title: Quasi-Newton ARC Optimizer — arcopt_qn
text: x0 Numeric vector initial parameter values (length n). fn Function computes objective function value. gr Function computes gradient. hess Optional Hessian function hybrid mode. provided, initializes B_0 = H(x_0) can refresh approximation degrades. ... Additional arguments passed fn, gr, hess. lower Numeric vector lower bounds (length n). Default: -Inf. upper Numeric vector upper bounds (length n). Default: Inf. control List control parameters (see Details).

title: Quasi-Newton ARC Optimizer — arcopt_qn
text: structure arcopt, plus: qn_updates: Number successful QN updates qn_skips: Number skipped updates qn_restarts: Number approximation restarts

title: Quasi-Newton ARC Optimizer — arcopt_qn
text: QN-ARC algorithm maintains quasi-Newton Hessian approximation B_k solves cubic regularization subproblem: $$m_k(s) = f_k + g_k^T s + 1/2 s^T B_k s + sigma_k/3 ||s||^3$$

title: Quasi-Newton ARC Optimizer — arcopt_qn
text: addition standard arcopt controls: qn_method: Update method (default: "hybrid"): "hybrid": Tries BFGS, falls back SR1, Powell-damped BFGS "sr1": Symmetric Rank-1 (allows indefinite Hessians) "bfgs": Standard BFGS (maintains positive definiteness) "lbfgs": Limited-memory BFGS "lsr1": Limited-memory SR1 "lhybrid": Limited-memory hybrid (L-BFGS -> L-SR1 -> Powell) bfgs_tol: Curvature tolerance BFGS hybrid mode (default: 1e-10) qn_memory: History size limited-memory methods (default: 10) sr1_skip_tol: SR1 skip test tolerance (default: 1e-8) sr1_restart_threshold: Consecutive skips restart (default: 5) use_accel_qn: EXPERIMENTAL Enable Nesterov acceleration (default: FALSE). May improve convergence strongly convex problems can hurt performance nonconvex problems. Use caution.

title: Check Convergence Criteria (Stan-style) — check_convergence
text: Checks multiple convergence criteria following Stan's L-BFGS implementation. Termination occurs criterion satisfied, preventing premature termination ill-scaled problems ensuring robust detection.

title: Check Convergence Criteria (Stan-style) — check_convergence
code: check_convergence(   g_current,   f_current,   f_previous,   x_current,   x_previous,   iter,   tol_grad = 1e-08,   tol_rel_grad = 1e-06,   tol_obj = 1e-12,   tol_rel_obj = 1e-08,   tol_param = 1e-08,   max_iter = 1000 )

title: Check Convergence Criteria (Stan-style) — check_convergence
text: g_current Current gradient vector (length Q) f_current Current objective function value (scalar) f_previous Previous objective function value (scalar), NA first iteration x_current Current parameter vector (length Q) x_previous Previous parameter vector (length Q), NULL first iteration iter Current iteration number (0-indexed) tol_grad Absolute gradient tolerance tol_rel_grad Relative gradient tolerance tol_obj Absolute objective change tolerance tol_rel_obj Relative objective change tolerance tol_param Parameter change tolerance (infinity norm) max_iter Maximum iterations allowed

title: Check Convergence Criteria (Stan-style) — check_convergence
text: List two components: converged Logical; TRUE criterion satisfied reason Character; description criterion triggered, "" converged

title: Check Convergence Criteria (Stan-style) — check_convergence
text: function checks 6 convergence criteria order: max_iter: Iteration limit reached gradient_abs: max(abs(g)) < tol_grad gradient_rel: max(abs(g)) < tol_rel_grad * max(1, abs(f)) objective_abs: abs(f_current - f_previous) < tol_obj objective_rel: abs(f_current - f_previous) < tol_rel_obj * max(1, abs(f_current)) parameter: max(abs(x_current - x_previous)) < tol_param

text: Logical; TRUE values finite valid, FALSE NaN/Inf detected

text: simple check function. recovery logic (re-solving larger sigma) implemented main optimization loop. Checks : Function value finite (NaN Inf) gradient components finite

what: Detect Stagnation and Recommend Action — detect_stagnation
title: Detect Stagnation and Recommend Action — detect_stagnation
text: Monitors optimization progress detects stagnation based small consecutive steps function changes. Returns recommended action: continue, refresh Hessian, stop.

title: Detect Stagnation and Recommend Action — detect_stagnation
code: detect_stagnation(   step_norms,   f_values,   tol_step = 1e-12,   tol_f = 1e-14,   max_stagnant = 5,   is_qn = FALSE,   already_refreshed = FALSE )

title: Detect Stagnation and Recommend Action — detect_stagnation
text: step_norms Vector recent step norms (recent last) f_values Vector recent function values (recent last) tol_step Step size tolerance stagnation (default: 1e-12) tol_f Function change tolerance stagnation (default: 1e-14) max_stagnant Maximum consecutive stagnant iterations action (default: 5) is_qn Logical; TRUE using quasi-Newton (enables Hessian refresh option) already_refreshed Logical; TRUE Hessian already refreshed

title: Detect Stagnation and Recommend Action — detect_stagnation
text: Character; one "continue", "refresh_hessian", "stop"

title: Detect Stagnation and Recommend Action — detect_stagnation
text: Stagnation detected either: Step norms consecutively tol_step max_stagnant iterations Function changes consecutively tol_f max_stagnant iterations stagnation detected: using quasi-Newton already refreshed: return "refresh_hessian" Otherwise: return "stop" (declare convergence failure)

what: Build L-BFGS Compact Form Matrices — lbfgs_compact_form
title: Build L-BFGS Compact Form Matrices — lbfgs_compact_form
text: Constructs w c_mat matrices compact representation B = gamma * + w' c_mat w L-BFGS history.

title: Build L-BFGS Compact Form Matrices — lbfgs_compact_form
code: lbfgs_compact_form(history)

title: Build L-BFGS Compact Form Matrices — lbfgs_compact_form
text: history L-BFGS history update_lbfgs.

title: Build L-BFGS Compact Form Matrices — lbfgs_compact_form
text: List w (m x n matrix) c_mat (m x m matrix), NULL history empty.

title: Build L-BFGS Compact Form Matrices — lbfgs_compact_form
text: compact L-BFGS form uses simplified SR1-like formulation: B = gamma * + Psi (Psi' S)^-1 Psi' Psi = Y - gamma * S.

title: Build L-BFGS Compact Form Matrices — lbfgs_compact_form
text: Nocedal, J., & Wright, S. J. (2006). Numerical Optimization (2nd ed.). Springer. Section 7.2.

title: L-BFGS Two-Loop Recursion — lbfgs_multiply
text: Computes product B*g using two-loop recursion algorithm, B L-BFGS approximation represented history.

title: L-BFGS Two-Loop Recursion — lbfgs_multiply
code: lbfgs_multiply(history, g)

title: L-BFGS Two-Loop Recursion — lbfgs_multiply
text: history L-BFGS history list update_lbfgs. g Vector multiply B (typically gradient).

title: L-BFGS Two-Loop Recursion — lbfgs_multiply
text: product B*g numeric vector.

title: L-BFGS Two-Loop Recursion — lbfgs_multiply
text: two-loop recursion computes B*g O(mn) operations m history length n dimension, avoiding explicit formation n x n matrix B. algorithm: Backward loop: compute alpha_i update q Initial scaling: r = gamma * q Forward loop: update r using beta_i

title: L-BFGS Two-Loop Recursion — lbfgs_multiply
text: Nocedal, J., & Wright, S. J. (2006). Numerical Optimization (2nd ed.). Springer. Algorithm 7.4.

title: L-BFGS Hessian-Vector Product (B*v) — lbfgs_multiply_b
text: Computes Bv (Hessian approximation times vector) L-BFGS history. Note: different lbfgs_multiply computes Hv (inverse Hessian times vector).

title: L-BFGS Hessian-Vector Product (B*v) — lbfgs_multiply_b
code: lbfgs_multiply_b(history, v, gamma = NULL)

title: L-BFGS Hessian-Vector Product (B*v) — lbfgs_multiply_b
text: history L-BFGS history list components s, y, rho, gamma. Can NULL (returns gamma*v just v gamma available). v Vector multiply (length n). gamma Initial scaling. NULL, uses history$gamma 1.0.

title: L-BFGS Hessian-Vector Product (B*v) — lbfgs_multiply_b
text: B*v B L-BFGS Hessian approximation.

title: L-BFGS Hessian-Vector Product (B*v) — lbfgs_multiply_b
text: Uses compact form representation: B = gammaI + Psi * inv(M) * Psi' Psi = Y - gammaS M = Psi' * S. needed Powell damping L-Hybrid, requires s^T B s.

title: Build L-SR1 Compact Form Matrices — lsr1_compact_form
text: List w (m x n matrix) c_mat (m x m matrix), NULL history empty.

title: Build L-SR1 Compact Form Matrices — lsr1_compact_form
text: L-SR1, compact form : B = gamma * + Psi * (Psi' S)^-1 * Psi' Psi = Y - gamma * S. can written B = gamma*+ w' c_mat w w = Psi' (w m x n) c_mat = (Psi' S)^-1.

title: L-SR1 Hessian-Vector Product — lsr1_multiply

title: L-SR1 Hessian-Vector Product — lsr1_multiply

title: L-SR1 Hessian-Vector Product — lsr1_multiply
text: product B*v numeric vector.

title: L-SR1 Hessian-Vector Product — lsr1_multiply
text: L-SR1 Hessian compact form: $$B = \gamma + \Psi (\Psi^T S)^{-1} \Psi^T$$ \(\Psi = Y - \gamma S\) S, Y matrices stored vectors. function computes B*v O(m^2 + mn) operations.

what: Solve Cubic Subproblem via Eigendecomposition (Algorithm 5a) — solve_cubic_eigen
title: Solve Cubic Subproblem via Eigendecomposition (Algorithm 5a) — solve_cubic_eigen
text: Solves min_s m(s) = g^T s + 1/2 s^T H s + sigma/3 ||s||^3 using full eigendecomposition explicit hard-case detection.

title: Solve Cubic Subproblem via Eigendecomposition (Algorithm 5a) — solve_cubic_eigen
code: solve_cubic_eigen(   g,   H,   sigma,   max_lambda_iter = 50,   lambda_tol = 1e-10,   hard_case_tol = 1e-08 )

title: Solve Cubic Subproblem via Eigendecomposition (Algorithm 5a) — solve_cubic_eigen
text: g Gradient vector (length n) H Hessian matrix (n x n symmetric, may indefinite) sigma Regularization parameter (positive scalar) max_lambda_iter Maximum secular equation iterations (default: 50) lambda_tol Secular equation convergence tolerance (default: 1e-10) hard_case_tol Hard case detection threshold (default: 1e-8)

title: Solve Cubic Subproblem via Eigendecomposition (Algorithm 5a) — solve_cubic_eigen
text: List : s Solution vector lambda Lagrange multiplier pred_reduction Predicted reduction: -g^T s - 1/2 s^T H s - sigma/3 ||s||^3 converged TRUE secular equation converged case_type "easy", "hard", "zero_gradient"

title: Solve Cubic Subproblem via Eigendecomposition (Algorithm 5a) — solve_cubic_eigen
text: Uses eigen() O(n^3) eigendecomposition. Hard case occurs gradient nearly orthogonal smallest eigenvector (|g_1| < tol_hard * ||g||). Recommended n <= 500 due cubic computational cost.

what: Simple Cubic Solver for Scaled Identity Hessian — solve_cubic_simple
title: Simple Cubic Solver for Scaled Identity Hessian — solve_cubic_simple
text: Solves cubic subproblem B = gamma * (QN updates). Uses closed-form solution: s = -g / (gamma + lambda) ||s|| = lambda / sigma.

title: Simple Cubic Solver for Scaled Identity Hessian — solve_cubic_simple
code: solve_cubic_simple(g, gamma, sigma, tol = 1e-10, max_iter = 50L)

text: List s, lambda, iterations, converged.

title: Solve Cubic Subproblem with Automatic Solver Selection — solve_cubic_subproblem_dispatch
text: List : s Solution vector lambda Lagrange multiplier pred_reduction Predicted reduction converged TRUE solver converged solver_used Name solver used

title: Solve Cubic Subproblem with Automatic Solver Selection — solve_cubic_subproblem_dispatch
text: Uses eigendecomposition-based solver (Algorithm 5a) provides robust handling indefinite Hessians hard cases. large-scale matrix-free optimization (n > 500), see design/scalable-arcs.qmd deferred large-scale solver implementations.

title: Woodbury-Based Cubic Subproblem Solver — solve_cubic_woodbury
text: Solves cubic regularization subproblem Hessian approximation low-rank structure quasi-Newton updates: B = gamma * + w' c_mat w.

title: Woodbury-Based Cubic Subproblem Solver — solve_cubic_woodbury
code: solve_cubic_woodbury(g, gamma, w, c_mat, sigma, tol = 1e-10, max_iter = 50L)

title: Woodbury-Based Cubic Subproblem Solver — solve_cubic_woodbury
text: g Gradient vector (length n). gamma Positive scalar initial Hessian scaling (B0 = gamma * ). w Low-rank update matrix (m x n, m << n). NULL, uses eigendecomposition fallback. c_mat Small coefficient matrix (m x m, symmetric). May indefinite SR1 updates. sigma Positive cubic regularization parameter. tol Convergence tolerance secular equation (default: 1e-10). max_iter Maximum Newton/bisection iterations (default: 50).

title: Woodbury-Based Cubic Subproblem Solver — solve_cubic_woodbury
text: list components: s Step vector (length n). lambda Regularization parameter satisfying secular equation. iterations Number iterations used. converged Logical indicating convergence.

title: Woodbury-Based Cubic Subproblem Solver — solve_cubic_woodbury
text: limited-memory quasi-Newton methods (L-BFGS, L-SR1), Hessian approximation form B = gamma * + w' c_mat w w contains secant vectors c_mat small m x m matrix. solver uses Woodbury identity compute: (B + lambda * )^-1 g = (1/(gamma + lambda)) g - (1/(gamma + lambda)^2) w' M^-1 w g M = c_mat^-1 + (1/(gamma + lambda)) w w'. Complexity O(m^2 n + m^3 log(1/tol)) vs O(n^3) eigendecomposition.

title: Woodbury-Based Cubic Subproblem Solver — solve_cubic_woodbury
text: Algorithm 5b design/pseudocode.qmd

title: Try Newton Step — try_newton_step

code: try_newton_step(g, H)

title: Try Newton Step — try_newton_step
text: g Gradient vector (length n) H Hessian matrix (n x n symmetric matrix)

title: Try Newton Step — try_newton_step
text: List components: success Logical; TRUE Newton step computed successfully s Newton step vector successful, NULL otherwise pred_reduction Predicted reduction quadratic model successful, NULL otherwise

title: Try Newton Step — try_newton_step
text: algorithm: Attempts Cholesky factorization H (fails H indefinite) successful, solves H * s = -g Newton step Computes predicted reduction quadratic model pre-filtering based sigma required - ratio test main ARC loop naturally determines whether Newton steps appropriate. See literature/consensus_reviews/explicit σ-threshold Newton Step.txt

title: BFGS (Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno) Hessian Update — update_bfgs
text: Updates Hessian approximation using BFGS formula. BFGS maintains positive definiteness curvature condition y'*s > 0 satisfied.

title: BFGS (Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno) Hessian Update — update_bfgs
code: update_bfgs(b, s, y)

title: BFGS (Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno) Hessian Update — update_bfgs
text: b Current Hessian approximation (n x n symmetric positive definite matrix). s Step vector: s = x_new - x_old. y Gradient difference: y = g_new - g_old.

title: BFGS (Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno) Hessian Update — update_bfgs
text: list components: b Updated Hessian approximation (n x n matrix). skipped Logical indicating update skipped due curvature condition violation.

title: BFGS (Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno) Hessian Update — update_bfgs
text: BFGS update formula : $$B_{k+1} = B_k - \frac{B_k s s^T B_k}{s^T B_k s} + \frac{y y^T}{y^T s}$$ update skipped curvature condition \(y^T s > 0\) violated. can happen nonconvex problems. skipped, previous approximation retained. Note: BFGS maintains positive definiteness, wastes ARC's ability handle indefinite Hessians. Consider using SR1 nonconvex problems.

title: BFGS (Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno) Hessian Update — update_bfgs
text: Algorithm 4a design/pseudocode.qmd Nocedal, J., & Wright, S. J. (2006). Numerical Optimization (2nd ed.). Springer. Section 6.1.

title: Powell-Damped BFGS Update — update_bfgs_powell
text: Applies Powell damping ensure curvature condition satisfied, performs BFGS update. guarantees update even standard curvature condition y's > 0 fails.

title: Powell-Damped BFGS Update — update_bfgs_powell
code: update_bfgs_powell(b, s, y, damping_threshold = 0.2)

title: Powell-Damped BFGS Update — update_bfgs_powell
text: b Current Hessian approximation (n x n symmetric matrix). s Step vector: s = x_new - x_old. y Gradient difference: y = g_new - g_old. damping_threshold Threshold damping (default: 0.2). Damping applied y's < damping_threshold * s'Bs.

title: Powell-Damped BFGS Update — update_bfgs_powell
text: list components: b Updated Hessian approximation (n x n matrix). theta Damping parameter used (1.0 = damping). y_damped damped y vector used update. skipped Logical indicating update skipped (s'Bs small apply damping).

title: Powell-Damped BFGS Update — update_bfgs_powell
text: Powell damping modifies y y_damped = theta*y + (1-theta)*Bs s'*y_damped >= damping_threshold * s'*Bs, ensuring positive curvature. formula theta y's < damping_threshold * s'Bs : theta = (1 - damping_threshold) * s'Bs / (s'Bs - y's) ensures s'*y_damped = damping_threshold * s'Bs exactly.

title: Powell-Damped BFGS Update — update_bfgs_powell
text: Powell, M. J. D. (1978). fast algorithm nonlinearly constrained optimization calculations. Numerical Analysis, 144-157. Algorithm 4a-hybrid design/pseudocode.qmd

what: Hybrid BFGS/SR1 Update with Automatic Routing — update_hybrid
title: Hybrid BFGS/SR1 Update with Automatic Routing — update_hybrid
text: Attempts BFGS first, falls back SR1, Powell-damped BFGS. Provides robust quasi-Newton updates convex nonconvex regions.

title: Hybrid BFGS/SR1 Update with Automatic Routing — update_hybrid
code: update_hybrid(   b,   s,   y,   bfgs_tol = 1e-10,   sr1_skip_tol = 1e-08,   skip_count = 0L,   restart_threshold = 5L )

title: Hybrid BFGS/SR1 Update with Automatic Routing — update_hybrid
text: b Current Hessian approximation (n x n symmetric matrix). s Step vector: s = x_new - x_old. y Gradient difference: y = g_new - g_old. bfgs_tol Tolerance BFGS curvature condition (default: 1e-10). sr1_skip_tol Tolerance SR1 skip test (default: 1e-8). skip_count Current count consecutive skipped updates. restart_threshold Number consecutive skips restart (default: 5).

title: Hybrid BFGS/SR1 Update with Automatic Routing — update_hybrid
text: list components: b Updated Hessian approximation (n x n matrix). update_type Character: "bfgs", "sr1", "powell", "skipped". skipped Logical indicating update applied. skip_count Updated skip counter (reset successful update). restarted Logical indicating b reset scaled identity. theta Powell damping parameter (update_type = "powell").

title: Hybrid BFGS/SR1 Update with Automatic Routing — update_hybrid
text: Routing priority: BFGS y's > bfgs_tol (stable, positive definite) SR1 |r's| >= sr1_skip_tol * ||r|| * ||s|| (allows indefiniteness) Powell-damped BFGS otherwise (guaranteed update) hybrid approach ideal problems transition convex nonconvex regions.

title: Hybrid BFGS/SR1 Update with Automatic Routing — update_hybrid
text: Algorithm 4a-hybrid design/pseudocode.qmd

text: Updated history list components: s List step vectors (recent last). y List gradient differences. rho Vector 1/(y'*s) values pair. gamma Scaling factor initial Hessian: (y'*s)/(y'*y).

title: L-BFGS History Update — update_lbfgs
text: L-BFGS stores m recent (s, y) pairs represents Hessian implicitly. scaling gamma Barzilai-Borwein estimate recent pair.

text: history L-Hybrid history list s, y, gamma. Can NULL. s Step vector: s = x_new - x_old. y Gradient difference: y = g_new - g_old. m Maximum number pairs store (default: 10). bfgs_tol BFGS curvature tolerance (default: 1e-10). sr1_skip_tol SR1 skip threshold (default: 1e-8). damping_threshold Powell damping threshold (default: 0.2).

title: Limited-Memory Hybrid BFGS/SR1 Update — update_lhybrid
text: list components: history Updated history structure s, y, gamma. update_type "lbfgs", "lsr1", "powell". theta Damping parameter (1.0 damping applied).

title: Limited-Memory Hybrid BFGS/SR1 Update — update_lhybrid
text: Routing priority: L-BFGS y'*s > bfgs_tol (stable, positive definite) L-SR1 SR1 denominator OK (allows indefiniteness) Powell-damped L-BFGS (guaranteed update) Uses unified history format (s, y, gamma) compatible lbfgs_compact_form lbfgs_multiply_b. use rho since need two-loop recursion (lbfgs_multiply). Algorithm 4a-lhybrid design/pseudocode.qmd.

code: update_lsr1(history, s, y, m = 10L, skip_tol = 1e-08, b_times_s = NULL)

title: L-SR1 History Update — update_lsr1
text: history List components s, y, gamma. Can NULL. s New step vector: s = x_new - x_old. y New gradient difference: y = g_new - g_old. m Maximum number pairs store (default: 10). skip_tol Tolerance skip test (default: 1e-8). b_times_s Product Bs B current approximation. NULL, uses gammas.

title: L-SR1 History Update — update_lsr1
text: Updated history list components: s List step vectors. y List gradient differences. gamma Scaling factor initial Hessian. skipped Logical indicating update skipped.

title: L-SR1 History Update — update_lsr1
text: Unlike L-BFGS, L-SR1 can represent indefinite Hessians uses different skip test based denominator SR1 formula.

title: Update Regularization Parameter (Classical CGT) — update_sigma_cgt
text: Adaptively adjusts regularization parameter sigma based well cubic model predicted actual function decrease. Follows classical Cartis-Gould-Toint (2011) update strategy.

title: Update Regularization Parameter (Classical CGT) — update_sigma_cgt
code: update_sigma_cgt(   sigma_current,   rho,   eta1 = 0.1,   eta2 = 0.9,   gamma1 = 0.5,   gamma2 = 2,   sigma_min = 1e-16,   sigma_max = 1e+16 )

title: Update Regularization Parameter (Classical CGT) — update_sigma_cgt
text: sigma_current Current regularization parameter (positive scalar) rho Ratio actual predicted reduction (scalar) eta1 Acceptance threshold (default: 0.1). Steps rho >= eta1 accepted. eta2 successful threshold (default: 0.9). Steps rho >= eta2 trigger sigma decrease. gamma1 Sigma decrease factor successful steps (default: 0.5) gamma2 Sigma increase factor unsuccessful steps (default: 2.0) sigma_min Minimum allowed sigma (default: 1e-16) sigma_max Maximum allowed sigma (default: 1e16)

title: Update Regularization Parameter (Classical CGT) — update_sigma_cgt
text: Updated sigma value (scalar)

title: Update Regularization Parameter (Classical CGT) — update_sigma_cgt
text: update strategy : successful (rho >= eta2): Decrease sigma gamma1 factor (trust model ) Moderately successful (eta1 <= rho < eta2): Keep sigma unchanged Unsuccessful (rho < eta1): Increase sigma gamma2 factor (trust model less) ratio rho measures model quality: rho = (f(x) - f(x+s)) / pred_reduction. Values near 1 indicate excellent model accuracy; values near 0 negative indicate poor model fit.

title: Update Regularization Parameter (Interpolation-Enhanced CGT) — update_sigma_interpolation
text: Adaptively adjusts sigma using interpolation estimate local Lipschitz constant model-function discrepancy. May provide faster adaptation large-scale problems varying curvature (Gould, Porcelli, Toint, 2012).

title: Update Regularization Parameter (Interpolation-Enhanced CGT) — update_sigma_interpolation
code: update_sigma_interpolation(   sigma_current,   rho,   s,   f_trial,   m_trial,   eta1 = 0.1,   eta2 = 0.9,   gamma1 = 0.5,   gamma2 = 2,   sigma_min = 1e-16,   sigma_max = 1e+16 )

title: Update Regularization Parameter (Interpolation-Enhanced CGT) — update_sigma_interpolation
text: sigma_current Current regularization parameter (positive scalar) rho Ratio actual predicted reduction (scalar) s Current step vector f_trial Function value trial point f(x + s) m_trial Model value step m(s) eta1 Acceptance threshold (default: 0.1) eta2 successful threshold (default: 0.9) gamma1 Sigma decrease factor successful steps (default: 0.5) gamma2 Sigma increase factor unsuccessful steps (default: 2.0) sigma_min Minimum allowed sigma (default: 1e-16) sigma_max Maximum allowed sigma (default: 1e16)

title: Update Regularization Parameter (Interpolation-Enhanced CGT) — update_sigma_interpolation
text: Updated sigma value (scalar)

title: Update Regularization Parameter (Interpolation-Enhanced CGT) — update_sigma_interpolation
text: Estimates local Lipschitz constant discrepancy function model: L_hat = 2 * |f(x+s) - m(s)| / ||s||^3 update strategy incorporates estimate: successful (rho >= eta2): sigma = max(L_hat/2, sigma/gamma1, sigma_min) Moderately successful (eta1 <= rho < eta2): Keep sigma unchanged Unsuccessful (rho < eta1): sigma = min(max(L_hat/2, gamma2*sigma), sigma_max) may provide better adaptation Algorithm 2a (classical CGT) curvature varies significantly across domain.

title: SR1 (Symmetric Rank-1) Hessian Update — update_sr1
text: Updates Hessian approximation using SR1 formula. Unlike BFGS, SR1 can represent indefinite matrices, making ideal ARC naturally handles negative curvature.

title: SR1 (Symmetric Rank-1) Hessian Update — update_sr1
code: update_sr1(b, s, y, skip_tol = 1e-08, skip_count = 0L, restart_threshold = 5L)

title: SR1 (Symmetric Rank-1) Hessian Update — update_sr1
text: b Current Hessian approximation (n x n symmetric matrix). s Step vector: s = x_new - x_old. y Gradient difference: y = g_new - g_old. skip_tol Tolerance skip test (default: 1e-8). Update skipped |r'*s| < skip_tol * ||r|| * ||s||. skip_count Current count consecutive skipped updates. restart_threshold Number consecutive skips restart (default: 5).

title: SR1 (Symmetric Rank-1) Hessian Update — update_sr1
text: list components: b Updated Hessian approximation (n x n matrix). skipped Logical indicating update skipped. skip_count Updated skip counter (reset 0 update applied restart triggered). restarted Logical indicating b reset scaled identity.

title: SR1 (Symmetric Rank-1) Hessian Update — update_sr1
text: SR1 update formula : $$B_{k+1} = B_k + \frac{r r^T}{r^T s}$$ \(r = y - B_k s\). update skipped denominator small relative norms r s, prevents numerical instability. many consecutive skips, approximation reset using Barzilai-Borwein scaling.

title: SR1 (Symmetric Rank-1) Hessian Update — update_sr1
text: Algorithm 4a design/pseudocode.qmd Conn, . R., Gould, N. ., & Toint, P. L. (1991). Convergence quasi-Newton matrices generated symmetric rank one update. Mathematical Programming, 50(1-3), 177-195.

text: Vector equal (B + lambda*) inverse times s.
